name: Pico Moncayo – trail running desde Cuevas de Ágreda
region_intro: Jogging routes around Cueva De Ágreda are set within a naturally beautiful environment, characterized by the imposing Moncayo Massif and the serene Araviana Valley. The region features ancient forests, clear streams, and a karstic landscape with unique geological formations. At an elevation of around 1,301 meters (4,268 feet), the area offers varied terrain suitable for outdoor activities. This setting provides a diverse range of running experiences, from challenging mountain trails to more gentle paths through oak woodlands. Best jogging routes around Cueva De Ágreda The most popular jogging route is Pico Moncayo – trail running desde Cuevas de Ágreda, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging ascent towards the Moncayo summit. Another top favourite among local runners is Moncayo Circular Trail, a difficult 6.2 miles (9.9 km) path. This circular route provides extensive views of the Moncayo Massif and its surrounding natural park. Local runners also love the Cabezo de la Mata — circular by the S-2 Barranco del Apio trail, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 43 minutes. Jogging around Cueva De Ágreda is defined by the Moncayo Massif, ancient oak forests, and karstic formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Cueva De Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Cueva De Ágreda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cueva de Ágreda?
A: There are over 30 running routes around Cueva de Ágreda, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 9 moderate paths and 23 more challenging mountain ascents, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Cueva de Ágreda?
A: Due to its high elevation of around 1,301 meters (4,268 feet), Cueva de Ágreda generally offers cool weather and clear views, making it pleasant for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cueva de Ágreda?
A: Yes, several excellent circular running routes are available. A popular choice is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983203586/moncayo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a challenging 6.2-mile (9.9 km) path offering extensive views of the Moncayo Massif. Another option is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983199672/cabezo-de-la-mata-circular-by-the-s-2-barranco-del-apio-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata — circular by the S-2 Barranco del Apio trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a moderate 3.2-mile (5.2 km) route through varied terrain.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running options near Cueva de Ágreda?
A: Absolutely. Cueva de Ágreda is an excellent destination for challenging trail running, with 23 difficult routes available. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064207420/pico-moncayo-trail-running-desde-cuevas-de-agreda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Moncayo – trail running desde Cuevas de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is a prime example, a 7.4-mile (11.9 km) route with a significant ascent towards the Moncayo summit.

Q: Are there running routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in the region provide stunning scenic viewpoints, especially those ascending the Moncayo Massif. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983203586/moncayo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, for instance, offers extensive panoramic views of the natural park and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any picnic areas or places to rest along the running trails?
A: Yes, the region offers pleasant spots for rest and picnics. The Dehesa de Cueva de Ágreda, about 2 kilometers from the village, features "Los Praos," an idyllic spot with meadows, ancient oak trees, and a dedicated picnic area. You can also find the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5249052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Sacristán Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; along some routes.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while running in Cueva de Ágreda?
A: The running routes in Cueva de Ágreda are rich in natural beauty. You can explore the imposing Moncayo Massif, the serene Araviana Valley with its karstic formations, and ancient oak forests. Notable geological features include "Las Patadas del Diablo" and impressive sinkholes like Las Simas. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; itself is a major attraction.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks near the running paths?
A: Yes, Cueva de Ágreda has several cultural points of interest. You can visit the 12th-century Romanesque &lt;strong&gt;Church of Santa María de la Cueva&lt;/strong&gt;, built upon a pre-Roman castro. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel Arcángel of Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is also nearby. Additionally, the village hosts an Interpretation Center of Moncayo and Bats, offering insights into the local ecosystem.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Cueva de Ágreda?
A: The running routes in Cueva de Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the region's pristine natural environment, the challenging yet rewarding mountain ascents, and the extensive views of the Moncayo Massif and its surrounding natural park.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by unique geological formations?
A: Yes, the karstic landscape of Cueva de Ágreda features several unique geological formations. Routes like the PR-SO 18 specifically take visitors through sites such as Las Simas (including Simonda, Similla, and Sima de los Trabucos) and the intriguing "Las Patadas del Diablo," grooves carved into the stone by water erosion.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, families can enjoy gentler sections, particularly in areas like the Dehesa de Cueva de Ágreda. The PR-SO 19, for example, traverses "Los Praos" through ancient oak forests, offering a scenic and less strenuous environment suitable for a family outing or a relaxed run.

Q: Are there options for longer distance running or trail races?
A: For longer distance running, the GR-260, known as "La Calcenada," is a significant 104 km route that circles the Moncayo. This trail is famous for hosting an annual race, indicating its suitability for long-distance running and providing an excellent challenge for experienced runners.
tips: At this point the two paths by which you can climb Moncayo from Cuevas de Ágreda come together. Normally, you go up the one on the right, through the Barranco del Colladico.

Located at an altitude of 2,314.3 meters, we arrive at Pico Moncayo, within the Natural Park of the same name. The panoramic views are splendid, but the route to the peak is also splendid, passing through the beautiful oak forest. Do not forget your coat, in any season the wind blows in Moncayo!
